pairs.question: Which stadium hosted the 1958 FIFA World Cup final?
pairs.answer: The 1958 FIFA World Cup final was held at Råsunda Stadium in Solna, near Stockholm, Sweden.

pairs.question: What was the final score of the 1958 FIFA World Cup match between Brazil and Sweden?
pairs.answer: Brazil defeated Sweden in the 1958 FIFA World Cup final. The specific score is not provided in the given text, but Brazil won the match to claim their first World Cup title.

pairs.question: What notable records were set during the 1958 FIFA World Cup final?
pairs.answer: The 1958 final holds the record for the most goals scored in a World Cup Final and shares the record for the greatest winning margin with the 1970 and 1998 tournaments. Additionally, it featured the youngest (Pelé, 17 years and 249 days) and oldest (Nils Liedholm, 35 years, 263 days) goalscorers in a World Cup final.

pairs.question: How did the 1958 FIFA World Cup final break historical precedents?
pairs.answer: This match was the first World Cup final to be played between a European team and a team from the Americas. It also marked the first and only time a World Cup hosted in Europe was not won by a European nation, as Brazil (from the Americas) claimed the title.

pairs.question: What unique distinction does Sweden hold regarding the 1958 FIFA World Cup final?
pairs.answer: Sweden became the first, and so far the only, host country to lose a World Cup Final. Their defeat in 1958 remains their best-ever World Cup finish.

pairs.question: Who were the last surviving players from the 1958 FIFA World Cup final, and when did they pass away?
pairs.answer: The last surviving player from Brazil's side was Mário Zagallo, who died on 5 January 2024 at the age of 92. The last survivor of the match overall, Sweden's Kurt Hamrin, died on 4 February 2024 at the age of 89.

pairs.question: Write a Python snippet to calculate the age difference between Pelé and Nils Liedholm at the time of the 1958 FIFA World Cup final.
pairs.answer:
python
# Ages at the time of the 1958 World Cup final
pele_age = 17 + (249 / 365)  # 17 years and 249 days
liedholm_age = 35 + (263 / 365)  # 35 years and 263 days

age_difference = liedholm_age - pele_age
print(f"Age difference: {age_difference:.2f} years")

This code calculates that Nils Liedholm was approximately 17.77 years older than Pelé during the 1958 final.
